# Fixture Ideation Evidence

The fixture began with three possible product loops: a public inspection catalogue, a guest favorite list, and a private collection organizer. The inspection catalogue was selected because it can demonstrate a complete entry, selection, detail, failure, and recovery loop without assuming account identity or hidden persistence.

The favorite-list direction was rejected for the first version because saving implies durable ownership and an account or device model. The private organizer was rejected because privacy, authorization, editing, and cross-device synchronization would dominate the product contract. These remain future hypotheses rather than controls that may quietly appear in the current interface.

The selected concept assumes guests value fast inspection and understandable recovery. That is a planning assumption, not target-user evidence. A later prototype test must observe whether guests recognize the public catalogue, select an item confidently, understand a load failure, and expect retry to preserve the same item.
